assignment_summary: Improve a supplied VeSync copywriting exercise draft for Cosori TurboBlaze across two Meta ad sets, three website banners, and one short email.

evidence_policy.generation_rule: Use this packet as the research boundary. Do not invent product features or live-source facts.

source_records.title: VeSync
source_records.evidence_snippet: VeSync identifies Cosori as one of its family brands.

source_records.title: TurboBlaze 6.0-Quart Air Fryer - Feature Section
source_records.evidence_snippet: up to 46% faster; up to 95% less oil.

source_records.title: TurboBlaze 6.0-Quart Air Fryer - Cleanup and Coating
source_records.evidence_snippet: PFAS-free ceramic coating; dishwasher-safe basket and crisper plate.

source_records.title: Cosori Air Fryer Collection
source_records.evidence_snippet: capacity, cooking functions, ease of cleaning.

source_records.title: Awareness Image Ad Specs on Facebook Feed
source_records.evidence_snippet: official specs and guidelines for Facebook Feed image ads.

source_records.title: Best practices for aspect ratios across placements
source_records.evidence_snippet: choose aspect ratios suited to placements and formats.

source_records.title: Meta Ads Library - Cosori examples not captured
source_records.evidence_snippet: No usable ad-card text captured for this staging packet.
used_for: do not claim actual Meta ad convention evidence

findings.claim: TurboBlaze is the strongest selected product for this exercise because it has concrete first-party feature claims and a low-risk wellness bridge around cooking routines.
findings.downstream_use: Anchor the creative strategy, but phrase wellness as behavior support rather than health outcome.

findings.claim: TurboBlaze verified feature stack: 6-quart capacity, 9-in-1 functions, up to 46% faster cooking, up to 95% less oil, PFAS-free ceramic coating, dishwasher-safe basket/crisper plate, 5 fan speeds, and automatic shutoff.
findings.downstream_use: Allowed in final copy when using 'up to' and the comparison basis where space permits.

findings.claim: The safest wellness bridge is routine formation: faster cooking, less oil, and easier cleanup reduce friction around cooking at home.
findings.downstream_use: Use for messaging territory. It is acceptable to say the product supports or contributes to a healthier, wellness-forward lifestyle through easier less-oil home cooking; do not state that the product guarantees health outcomes.

findings.claim: Cosori voice should stay food-first, practical, capable, and habit-oriented rather than clinical wellness language.
findings.downstream_use: Pressure-test final copy against food pleasure plus practical ease.

findings.claim: Current staged packet has platform constraints from Meta docs but does not yet have actual Cosori Ads Library examples. Since live research is allowed during generation, HoloBuild should attempt to capture current examples and store them before treating ad patterns as observed brand conventions.
findings.downstream_use: Label ad convention claims as channel best-practice/pattern hypotheses, not observed brand ad conventions.

findings.claim: Avoid disease, allergy, weight-loss, guilt-free, medical-grade, toxin-free, or guaranteed-health language.
findings.downstream_use: Hard safety boundary for the HoloBuild QA turns and solo judging rubric.

product_comparison_snapshot.why: Feature claims map cleanly to home-cooking routines, time savings, less-oil cooking, and cleanup ease.
product_comparison_snapshot.risk_notes: Use 'up to' claims. Avoid diet, weight-loss, guilt-free, and health-result language.

preferred_messaging_territories: home cooking that fits real routines
one appliance for multiple everyday meals
cleanup simple enough for repeat use
creative_guidance.mandatory_strategy_anchor: Every ad, banner, and email must make the feature-to-lifestyle bridge explicit: name the feature, show the friction it removes, and connect that to a better routine or wellness-forward lifestyle.
creative_guidance.turboblaze_healthier_lifestyle_strategy: TurboBlaze contributes to a healthier lifestyle by making less-oil, home-cooked meals easier to choose repeatedly. The copy should emphasize lighter crispy favorites, faster weeknight meals, more home-cooking momentum, and cleanup that supports repeat use.
draft_improvement_priorities: Make the copy less feature-dump and more channel-native.
Keep 46% faster and 95% less oil but preserve 'up to' and avoid unsupported outcomes.
Add stronger Cosori food pleasure and routine language.
Make the three banners operate as a sequence instead of repeating the same feature stack.
Shorten the email into a warmer 100-150 word introduction with one clear CTA and a non-redundant P.S.
known_initial_draft_risks: Some lines read like a spec sheet rather than paid/social ecommerce copy.
The phrase 'whole household' should be softened unless tied to the 3-5 person capacity wording.
Meta ad convention evidence is platform-constraint-only unless Ads Library examples are separately captured.

source_appendix_policy: Every final claim should retain a finding_id pointer if a later packet format supports citations. Final creative copy itself should not contain source IDs unless the evaluator asks for annotated output.
